description: Read comment thread for a Hacker News story. Extracts comments with author, text, score, and nesting depth. No login required for public threads.
author: stillwater

reasoning.research: HN comment threads at /item?id={story_id} render as pure HTML. Story details are in .fatitem .athing. Comments are in .comment-tree. Each comment is a tr.athing[id] containing .ind (indentation pixel width for depth), .hnuser (author), .commtext (text content), .age a (timestamp), .score (points, only for users with karma >= 500). Depth is calculable from .ind img[width] attribute (each 40px = 1 depth level).
reasoning.selector_strategy: Navigate to news.ycombinator.com/item?id={story_id}. Wait for .comment-tree. Extract .athing.comtr for all comments. .commtext .c00 for text (or .commtext directly). .hnuser for author. Depth from .ind img width / 40. No login needed.
reasoning.anti_bot_notes: HN comment pages are pure public HTML. Zero bot detection. Fast to load (< 50KB for typical thread). Very high selector confidence due to 15-year stable HTML structure.
reasoning.performance: Navigation 2s + extraction 2s = ~4s total. One of the fastest recipes in the collection.

limit.description: Maximum number of comments to extract

steps.description: Navigate to HN story/comment page (no login required)
Wait for comment tree to load
Return extracted comments as structured data

story_title.example: Show HN: My open source browser automation tool

error_handling.session_expired: HN comments are public — session not required. This error should not occur.
error_handling.story_not_found: Detect 'No such item' — story may have been killed or ID is wrong
error_handling.comments_disabled: Return empty comments array — story may be new or have 0 comments
error_handling.selector_timeout: Retry once with 12s timeout on .fatitem, then abort
